OS Interface File Virtual Methods Object.

Every file opened by the sqlite3_vfs.xOpen method populates an sqlite3_file object (or, more commonly, a subclass of the sqlite3_file object) with a pointer to an instance of this object. This object defines the methods used to perform various operations against the open file represented by the sqlite3_file object.

If the sqlite3_vfs.xOpen method sets the sqlite3_file.pMethods element to a non-NULL pointer, then the sqlite3_io_methods.xClose method may be invoked even if the sqlite3_vfs.xOpen reported that it failed. The only way to prevent a call to xClose following a failed sqlite3_vfs.xOpen is for the sqlite3_vfs.xOpen to set the sqlite3_file.pMethods element to NULL.

The flags argument to xSync may be one of SQLITE_SYNC_NORMAL or SQLITE_SYNC_FULL. The first choice is the normal fsync(). The second choice is a Mac OS X style fullsync. The SQLITE_SYNC_DATAONLY flag may be ORed in to indicate that only the data of the file and not its inode needs to be synced.

The integer values to xLock() and xUnlock() are one of SQLITE_LOCK_NONE, SQLITE_LOCK_SHARED, SQLITE_LOCK_RESERVED, SQLITE_LOCK_PENDING, or SQLITE_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE. xLock() upgrades the database file lock. In other words, xLock() moves the database file lock in the direction NONE toward EXCLUSIVE. The argument to xLock() is always one of SHARED, RESERVED, PENDING, or EXCLUSIVE, never SQLITE_LOCK_NONE. If the database file lock is already at or above the requested lock, then the call to xLock() is a no-op. xUnlock() downgrades the database file lock to either SHARED or NONE. If the lock is already at or below the requested lock state, then the call to xUnlock() is a no-op. The xCheckReservedLock() method checks whether any database connection, either in this process or in some other process, is holding a RESERVED, PENDING, or EXCLUSIVE lock on the file. It returns, via its output pointer parameter, true if such a lock exists and false otherwise.

The xFileControl() method is a generic interface that allows custom VFS implementations to directly control an open file using the sqlite3_file_control() interface. The second "op" argument is an integer opcode. The third argument is a generic pointer intended to point to a structure that may contain arguments or space in which to write return values. Potential uses for xFileControl() might be functions to enable blocking locks with timeouts, to change the locking strategy (for example to use dot-file locks), to inquire about the status of a lock, or to break stale locks. The SQLite core reserves all opcodes less than 100 for its own use. A list of opcodes less than 100 is available. Applications that define a custom xFileControl method should use opcodes greater than 100 to avoid conflicts. VFS implementations should return SQLITE_NOTFOUND for file control opcodes that they do not recognize.

The xSectorSize() method returns the sector size of the device that underlies the file. The sector size is the minimum write that can be performed without disturbing other bytes in the file. The xDeviceCharacteristics() method returns a bit vector describing behaviors of the underlying device:

- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC512
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC1K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC2K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC4K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC8K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC16K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC32K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC64K
- SQLITE_IOCAP_SAFE_APPEND
- SQLITE_IOCAP_SEQUENTIAL
- SQLITE_IOCAP_UNDELETABLE_WHEN_OPEN
- SQLITE_IOCAP_POWERSAFE_OVERWRITE
- SQLITE_IOCAP_IMMUTABLE
- SQLITE_IOCAP_BATCH_ATOMIC
- SQLITE_IOCAP_SUBPAGE_READ

The SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMIC property means that all writes of any size are atomic. The SQLITE_IOCAP_ATOMICnnn values mean that writes of blocks that are nnn bytes in size and are aligned to an address which is an integer multiple of nnn are atomic. The SQLITE_IOCAP_SAFE_APPEND value means that when data is appended to a file, the data is appended first then the size of the file is extended, never the other way around. The SQLITE_IOCAP_SEQUENTIAL property means that information is written to disk in the same order as calls to xWrite().

If xRead() returns SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ it must also fill in the unread portions of the buffer with zeros. A VFS that fails to zero-fill short reads might seem to work. However, failure to zero-fill short reads will eventually lead to database corruption.

to_generate.name: busy_handler
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to handle SQLITE_BUSY errors for the given database connection. The handler will be called with the invocation count for the current locking event. Returning 0 from the handler stops further attempts and causes SQLITE_BUSY to be returned; returning a nonzero value causes SQLite to retry. Passing an invalid or null Callable clears the busy handler. Only one busy handler can be set at a time. Setting a new handler replaces the previous one.

to_generate.name: fcntl_last_errno

to_generate.name: fcntl_size_hint

to_generate.name: fcntl_chunk_size

to_generate.name: fcntl_file_pointer

to_generate.name: fcntl_persist_wal

to_generate.name: fcntl_overwrite

to_generate.name: fcntl_vfsname
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_VFSNAME opcode can be used to obtain the names of all VFSes in the VFS stack.

to_generate.name: fcntl_powersafe_overwrite

to_generate.name: fcntl_pragma

to_generate.name: fcntl_busyhandler

to_generate.name: fcntl_tempfilename

to_generate.name: fcntl_mmap_size

to_generate.name: fcntl_trace

to_generate.name: fcntl_has_moved

to_generate.name: fcntl_sync

to_generate.name: fcntl_wal_block
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_WAL_BLOCK is a signal to the VFS layer that it might be advantageous to block on the next WAL lock if the lock is not immediately available.

to_generate.name: fcntl_zipvfs
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_ZIPVFS opcode is implemented by zipvfs only. All other VFS should return SQLITE_NOTFOUND for this opcode.

to_generate.name: fcntl_rbu
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_RBU opcode is implemented by the special VFS used by the RBU extension only.

to_generate.name: fcntl_vfs_pointer
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_VFS_POINTER opcode finds a pointer to the top-level VFS currently in use.

to_generate.name: fcntl_journal_pointer
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_JOURNAL_POINTER opcode obtains a pointer to the sqlite3_file object associated with the journal file for a particular database connection.

to_generate.name: fcntl_win32_get_handle
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_WIN32_GET_HANDLE opcode can be used to obtain the underlying native file handle associated with a file handle.

to_generate.name: fcntl_pdb
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_PDB opcode is reserved for future use.

to_generate.name: fcntl_begin_atomic_write
to_generate.doc: If the SQLITE_FCNTL_BEGIN_ATOMIC_WRITE opcode returns SQLITE_OK, then the file descriptor is placed in batch write mode for atomic writes.

to_generate.name: fcntl_commit_atomic_write
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_COMMIT_ATOMIC_WRITE opcode causes all write operations since the last begin atomic write to be performed atomically.

to_generate.name: fcntl_rollback_atomic_write
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_ROLLBACK_ATOMIC_WRITE opcode causes all write operations since the last begin atomic write to be rolled back.

to_generate.name: fcntl_lock_timeout
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_LOCK_TIMEOUT opcode configures a VFS to block for up to a specified number of milliseconds before failing when attempting to obtain a file lock.

to_generate.name: fcntl_data_version
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_DATA_VERSION opcode is used to detect changes to a database file via a pointer to a 32-bit unsigned integer.

to_generate.name: fcntl_size_limit
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_SIZE_LIMIT opcode is used by in-memory VFS to set an upper bound on the size of the in-memory database.

to_generate.name: fcntl_ckpt_done
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CKPT_DONE opcode is invoked from within a checkpoint in WAL mode after the client has finished copying pages from the WAL file.

to_generate.name: fcntl_reserve_bytes
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_RESERVE_BYTES opcode is reserved for future use.

to_generate.name: fcntl_ckpt_start
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CKPT_START opcode is invoked from within a checkpoint in WAL mode before the client starts to copy pages from the WAL file.

to_generate.name: fcntl_external_reader
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_EXTERNAL_READER opcode is used to detect whether there is a database client in another process with a WAL-mode transaction open.

to_generate.name: fcntl_cksm_file
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_CKSM_FILE opcode is for internal use by the checksum VFS shim only.

to_generate.name: fcntl_reset_cache
to_generate.doc: If there is currently no transaction open on the database, the SQLITE_FCNTL_RESET_CACHE file-control purges the contents of the in-memory page cache.

to_generate.name: fcntl_null_io
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_NULL_IO opcode sets the file descriptor so that it will no longer read or write to the database file.

to_generate.name: fcntl_block_on_connect
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_BLOCK_ON_CONNECT opcode is used to configure the VFS to block when taking a SHARED lock to connect to a WAL mode database.

to_generate.name: fcntl_filestat
to_generate.doc: The SQLITE_FCNTL_FILESTAT opcode returns low-level diagnostic information about sqlite3_file objects used to access database and journal files.

to_generate.name: static_destructor_type

to_generate.name: transient_destructor_type

to_generate.doc: Creates a new SQLite VFS filename handle using the specified database, journal, WAL filenames, and key/value URI parameters. The returned handle must be freed using sqlite3_free_filename.

to_generate.name: value_blob
to_generate.doc: Returns the BLOB value of the provided sqlite3_value as a PackedByteArray. Returns an empty array if the value is not of BLOB type or is NULL.

to_generate.name: bind_blob64
to_generate.doc: Binds a blob value to the specified parameter index in the prepared statement. The blob data is provided as a PackedByteArray. The destructor argument should be -1 for SQLITE_TRANSIENT (SQLite will copy the data) or 0 for SQLITE_STATIC (data is assumed to remain valid for the statement's lifetime). Returns a SQLite result code. Other values for destructor are unsupported and unsafe.
A wrapper class for the sqlite3_value handle.

to_generate.name: value_pointer
to_generate.doc: Returns the pointer stored in the sqlite3_value for the given type name, or null if not set.

to_generate.name: get_autocommit
to_generate.doc: Returns non-zero if the given database connection is in autocommit mode, zero otherwise. Autocommit mode is on by default, disabled by BEGIN, and re-enabled by COMMIT or ROLLBACK. Use this function to determine if a transaction was automatically rolled back after an error. The return value is undefined if another thread changes autocommit status during the call.

to_generate.name: soft_heap_limit
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Sets the soft heap limit for SQLite using the deprecated interface. Provided for historical compatibility only. New applications should use soft_heap_limit64 instead.

to_generate.name: db_release_memory
to_generate.doc: Attempts to free as much heap memory as possible from the given SQLite database connection.
A handle to an SQLite virtual table interface structure.

to_generate.name: hard_heap_limit64
to_generate.doc: Sets or queries the hard heap memory limit for SQLite in bytes. If n > 0, sets the limit to n and returns the previous value. If n == 0, returns the current limit.

to_generate.name: result_blob
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an application-defined SQL function to a BLOB value. If is_transient is true, SQLite will make a private copy of the data; if false, SQLite assumes the data pointer remains valid for its use.

to_generate.name: result_value
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQL function to the value of another sqlite3_value.

to_generate.name: set_auxdata
to_generate.doc: Sets auxiliary data for the given context and parameter index. The 'auxdata' Variant is passed to SQLite, and the 'destructor' Callable will be called with the auxdata when SQLite destroys it.

to_generate.name: result_double
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQLite user-defined function to the given double value.

to_generate.name: release_memory
to_generate.doc: Attempts to free up to N bytes of heap memory used by SQLite for non-essential allocations. Returns the number of bytes actually freed, which may be more or less than requested. Returns zero if memory management is not enabled.

to_generate.name: next_stmt
to_generate.doc: Returns the next prepared statement after 'stmt' associated with the database connection 'db'. If 'stmt' is null, returns the first prepared statement. Returns null if there are no more prepared statements.
A wrapper class for the sqlite3_index_info handle.

to_generate.name: reset_auto_extension
to_generate.doc: Disables all automatic extensions previously registered using sqlite3_auto_extension().
A wrapper class for the SQLite virtual table cursor handle.

to_generate.name: win32_data_directory_type

to_generate.name: win32_temp_directory_type

to_generate.name: sqlite3_win32_set_directory8
to_generate.doc: Sets a Win32 directory used by SQLite for temporary or database files. The 'type' parameter selects which directory is set. Returns a result code.

to_generate.name: collation_needed16
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked when a UTF-16 collation sequence is needed but not yet loaded. The callback is called with (db: Ref<Sqlite3Handle>, eTextRep: int, collation_name: String).

to_generate.name: win32_set_directory
to_generate.doc: Win32-only interface: Sets the value associated with the sqlite3_temp_directory or sqlite3_data_directory variable, depending on the type. Passing an empty string for z_value will reset (free) the previous value. The function returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_ERROR if the type is unsupported, or SQLITE_NOMEM if memory could not be allocated.

to_generate.name: get_temp_directory
to_generate.doc: Gets the current folder used for SQLite temporary files, or an empty string if unset. Use with caution: this is a global variable and is not thread-safe. Changing it after SQLite has been initialized or from multiple threads is unsafe.

to_generate.name: set_temp_directory
to_generate.doc: Sets the folder used for SQLite temporary files. Passing an empty string unsets the directory. This is a global variable and is not thread-safe; only set during process initialization and before any SQLite API calls. The string is copied and ownership is managed appropriately.

to_generate.name: create_collation_v2
to_generate.doc: Creates a new collation for the SQLite database. The 'compare' Callable will be called to compare two strings as part of the collation. The 'destroy' Callable (if valid) will be called when the collation is destroyed. The compare Callable should accept two PackedByteArray arguments and return an int (negative, zero, or positive).

to_generate.name: cancel_auto_extension
to_generate.doc: Unregisters the initialization routine previously registered using sqlite3_auto_extension. The argument x_entry_point must be a pointer to a function with signature void(void), cast to int64_t. Returns 1 if successfully unregistered, 0 otherwise.

to_generate.name: mutex_free
to_generate.doc: Frees an SQLite mutex object. After this call, the mutex handle is no longer valid.

to_generate.name: vfs_unregister
to_generate.doc: Unregisters the given VFS object.
A wrapper class for the SQLite mutex handle.

to_generate.name: mutex_enter
to_generate.doc: Enters (locks) the specified SQLite mutex.

to_generate.name: get_data_directory
to_generate.doc: Returns the directory name used by SQLite for database files with relative paths. If null, files are relative to the process's current directory. Only used by the Windows VFS; ignored by the Unix VFS. Not thread-safe. See C API docs for memory management caveats.

to_generate.name: set_data_directory
to_generate.doc: Sets the directory name used by SQLite for database files with relative paths. Only used by the Windows VFS; ignored by the Unix VFS. Not thread-safe. Memory pointed to by this variable may be freed by SQLite if certain pragmas are used; allocate only with malloc or SQLite's allocator. Changing this while a database is open may corrupt the database. See SQLite documentation for details.

to_generate.name: result_error16
to_generate.doc: Sets the error message for the context using a UTF-16 encoded string. The error_message is converted to UTF-16 and passed to SQLite. If nbytes < 0, the message is treated as null-terminated. If nbytes >= 0, only nbytes bytes are read.

to_generate.name: rollback_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked when a transaction is rolled back on the given database connection. The callback receives no arguments. Returns the previous user data pointer as int64_t (only meaningful if you chain hooks). Warning: The callback will be called from the thread performing the rollback; avoid blocking or accessing freed objects.

to_generate.name: db_mutex
to_generate.doc: Retrieves the mutex object that serializes access to the given SQLite database connection. Returns null if the threading mode is not serialized.

to_generate.name: mutex_leave

to_generate.name: keyword_count

to_generate.name: mutex_try

to_generate.name: vfs_register

to_generate.doc: Enable or disable the recording of changes by a session object. When enabled, a session object records changes made to the database. When disabled, it does not. Passing zero disables the session, a value greater than zero enables it, and a value less than zero is a no-op and can be used to query the current state. Returns 1 if the session is enabled or 0 if disabled.

to_generate.name: carray_bind_v2
to_generate.doc: Binds an array value to the first argument of the carray() table-valued function in SQLite. 'stmt' is the prepared statement handle. 'index' is the parameter index to bind. 'data' is the array to bind, and 'n_elements' is the number of elements in the array. 'flags' specifies the type of the array (one of SQLITE_CARRAY_INT32, SQLITE_CARRAY_INT64, SQLITE_CARRAY_DOUBLE, SQLITE_CARRAY_TEXT, or SQLITE_CARRAY_BLOB). 'x_del' should be SQLITE_STATIC or SQLITE_TRANSIENT to control the lifetime of the data (custom destructors are not supported; pass as integer constant). 'p_del' is ignored unless a custom destructor is used. Returns the result code from sqlite3_carray_bind_v2.

to_generate.name: session_create
to_generate.doc: Creates a new session object attached to the given SQLite database handle. The session object will be used to create changesets for tables in the specified database (z_db, e.g. "main"). On success, returns SQLITE_OK and sets out_session to the new session. On error, returns an SQLite error code and out_session is not modified. Session objects should be deleted before closing the database handle they are attached to. It is not possible to use pre-update hooks with a database while session objects are attached.

to_generate.name: close_v2
to_generate.doc: Closes the database connection. After this call, the database handle is no longer valid. Returns an SQLite status code.

to_generate.name: changeset_strm
to_generate.doc: Writes a changeset for the given session to a stream by invoking the provided callback for each output chunk. The callback receives a PackedByteArray containing the data buffer and must return an int status. Returning non-zero from the callback will stop streaming and return that value.

to_generate.name: set_last_insert_rowid

to_generate.name: changegroup_add_strm
to_generate.doc: Adds changes from a stream to the changegroup. The x_input Callable receives an int buffer size and must return a PackedByteArray with data to copy. Returns 0 on success, or an error code.

to_generate.name: total_changes
to_generate.doc: Returns the total number of rows inserted, modified, or deleted by all INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statements executed on this database connection since it was opened. See SQLite documentation for details.

to_generate.name: changes
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of rows modified, inserted, or deleted by the most recent INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statement on the given database connection. Only changes to real tables are counted; changes due to triggers or views are not included.

to_generate.name: changeset_start_v2_strm
to_generate.doc: Starts a changeset iterator from a stream using a custom input callback. The x_input callback receives one argument (requested buffer size) and must return a PackedByteArray (the data to provide). It may alternatively return an Array [PackedByteArray, int] to specify a custom result code. On success, out_iter is set to the new iterator.

to_generate.name: create
to_generate.doc: Creates a new changegroup object for combining changesets or patchsets. Returns the new object on success, or null on failure.

to_generate.name: setlk_block_on_connect

to_generate.name: last_insert_rowid
to_generate.doc: Returns the ROWID of the most recent successful INSERT into a rowid table for the given database connection. Returns 0 if no successful INSERTs have occurred.

to_generate.name: changeset_concat
to_generate.doc: Concatenates two changeset objects. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (the SQLite status code) and 'changeset' (the resulting changeset as a PackedByteArray, or empty if an error occurred). The result is equivalent to applying changeset A followed by changeset B.

to_generate.name: complete
to_generate.doc: Determines if the given SQL string forms a complete SQL statement. Returns 1 if complete, 0 if incomplete, or SQLITE_NOMEM on memory allocation error.

to_generate.name: interrupt
to_generate.doc: Interrupts any pending database operation on the given SQLite connection, causing it to abort as soon as possible. Safe to call from another thread. Do not call on a closed connection.

to_generate.name: changeset_concat_strm
to_generate.doc: Concatenates two changeset input streams into a single output stream using user-provided Godot Callables as I/O callbacks. Each input and output Callable receives a PackedByteArray buffer and a user context Variant. Input callbacks should return a Dictionary with keys 'data' (PackedByteArray), 'size' (int), and optionally 'result' (int). Output callback should return an int status code.

to_generate.name: busy_timeout
to_generate.doc: Sets a busy handler that sleeps for the specified number of milliseconds when a table is locked. Passing a value less than or equal to zero disables all busy handlers. Returns a SQLite status code.

to_generate.name: trace_stmt
to_generate.doc: Event code for when a prepared statement begins running.

to_generate.name: trace_profile
to_generate.doc: Event code for when a prepared statement finishes running, with profiling information.

to_generate.name: trace_row
to_generate.doc: Event code for when a prepared statement generates a result row.

to_generate.name: trace_close
to_generate.doc: Event code for when a database connection closes.
A wrapper class for a prepared SQL statement handle (sqlite3_stmt).

to_generate.name: setlk_timeout
to_generate.doc: Sets the setlk timeout (in milliseconds) for blocking locks on WAL-mode databases for the specified database handle. Only useful if built with SQLITE_ENABLE_SETLK_TIMEOUT and if the VFS supports blocking locks. Passing 0 disables blocking locks; passing -1 requests to block indefinitely. Call sqlite3_busy_timeout() first to set the busy timeout separately if desired. The flags parameter may include SQLITE_SETLK_BLOCK_ON_CONNECT.

to_generate.name: open
to_generate.doc: Opens an SQLite database file with the specified filename (UTF-8). On success, out_db receives the opened database handle. Returns SQLITE_OK or an error code.

to_generate.name: set_errmsg
to_generate.doc: Sets the error code and message for a database handle. If the error message is empty, the default message for the error code is used. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_NOMEM on OOM, or SQLITE_MISUSE if db is null or invalid.

to_generate.name: errmsg16
to_generate.doc: Returns the most recent error message for the given database as a String, converting from the UTF-16 encoded message returned by sqlite3_errmsg16.

to_generate.name: open_v2
to_generate.doc: Opens a SQLite database using the specified flags and VFS module name. On success, sets out_db to a valid handle. Returns 0 (SQLITE_OK) on success or an error code on failure.
Opaque handle for SQLite R-Tree geometry callback context.

to_generate.name: rtree_geometry_callback
to_generate.doc: Registers a geometry callback named z_geom for use in R-Tree queries. The x_geom Callable will be called during geometry queries with arguments (Sqlite3RtreeGeometryHandle, PackedFloat64Array). It should return either an int SQLite result code, or a Dictionary with 'result_code' and optionally 'output' for the output parameter.

to_generate.name: error_offset
to_generate.doc: Returns the byte offset of the syntax error in the most recent failed SQL statement, or -1 if not available.

to_generate.name: deserialize
to_generate.doc: Deserializes a database from the given buffer and attaches it to the specified database connection. Only the SQLITE_DESERIALIZE_READONLY flag is supported by this wrapper, because the buffer is a temporary copy and will not remain alive after this call. Passing other flags is not supported and will return SQLITE_MISUSE. The schema argument specifies which database to reopen (pass an empty string for the main database). See SQLite documentation for detailed semantics and restrictions. This function is unavailable if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_OMIT_DESERIALIZE.

to_generate.name: profile
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Installs a profile callback for the given database handle. The callback will be invoked after execution of each SQL statement, receiving the SQL string and elapsed time in nanoseconds. Returns the previous callback pointer as an integer. This API is deprecated in SQLite.

to_generate.name: activate_cerod
to_generate.doc: Specify the activation key for a CEROD database. Unless activated, none of the CEROD routines will work.

to_generate.name: txn_none
to_generate.doc: No transaction is currently pending.

to_generate.name: txn_read
to_generate.doc: The database is currently in a read transaction. Content may have been read but not changed.

to_generate.name: txn_write
to_generate.doc: The database is currently in a write transaction. Content has been written but not yet committed.

to_generate.name: db_handle
to_generate.doc: Returns the database connection handle to which the prepared statement belongs.

to_generate.name: sleep
to_generate.doc: Suspends execution of the current thread for at least the specified number of milliseconds. Returns the number of milliseconds actually slept.

to_generate.name: open16
to_generate.doc: Opens a SQLite database using a UTF-16 encoded filename. The provided filename String will be converted to UTF-16 automatically. On success, sets out_db to the new SQLite database handle and returns SQLITE_OK. On failure, returns a SQLite error code and out_db is not modified.

to_generate.name: get_table
to_generate.doc: Evaluates one or more semicolon-separated SQL statements and returns a result table as a PackedStringArray, along with the row count, column count, error message, and result code. The result table is a flat array: the first N columns are column names, followed by row results. This is a legacy interface preserved for backwards compatibility.

to_generate.name: enable_load_extension
to_generate.doc: Enables or disables extension loading for the SQLite database connection. Extension loading is off by default. Enabling it may have security implications. Prefer using the db_config interface when possible.
A wrapper for the SQLite3 virtual table instance handle. Used internally to represent virtual table objects.

to_generate.name: txn_state
to_generate.doc: Returns the current transaction state of the specified schema in the given database connection. If z_schema is empty, returns the highest transaction state of any schema on the connection. Returns 0 (SQLITE_TXN_NONE), 1 (SQLITE_TXN_READ), 2 (SQLITE_TXN_WRITE), or -1 if the schema name is invalid.

to_generate.name: db_readonly
to_generate.doc: Returns 1 if the database named db_name on connection db is read-only, 0 if it is read/write, or -1 if db_name is not the name of a database on the connection.

to_generate.name: db_filename
to_generate.doc: Returns the filename associated with the specified attached database name for the given SQLite connection. Returns an empty string if there is no such database or if the database is in-memory or temporary.

to_generate.name: enable_shared_cache
to_generate.doc: Enables or disables the sharing of the database cache and schema data structures between connections to the same database for future connections. Returns SQLITE_OK on success. This function is omitted if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_OMIT_SHARED_CACHE. Use of shared cache is discouraged.

to_generate.name: limit_length
to_generate.doc: The maximum size of any string or BLOB or table row, in bytes.

to_generate.name: limit_sql_length
to_generate.doc: The maximum length of an SQL statement, in bytes.

to_generate.name: limit_column
to_generate.doc: The maximum number of columns in a table definition or result set of a SELECT, or in an index or ORDER BY/GROUP BY clause.

to_generate.name: limit_expr_depth
to_generate.doc: The maximum depth of the parse tree on any expression.

to_generate.name: limit_compound_select
to_generate.doc: The maximum number of terms in a compound SELECT statement.

to_generate.name: limit_vdbe_op
to_generate.doc: The maximum number of instructions in a virtual machine program used to implement an SQL statement.

to_generate.name: limit_function_arg
to_generate.doc: The maximum number of arguments on a function.

to_generate.name: limit_attached
to_generate.doc: The maximum number of attached databases.

to_generate.name: limit_like_pattern_length
to_generate.doc: The maximum length of the pattern argument to the LIKE or GLOB operators.

to_generate.name: limit_variable_number
to_generate.doc: The maximum index number of any parameter in an SQL statement.

to_generate.name: limit_trigger_depth
to_generate.doc: The maximum depth of recursion for triggers.

to_generate.name: limit_worker_threads
to_generate.doc: The maximum number of auxiliary worker threads that a single prepared statement may start.

to_generate.name: limit_parser_depth
to_generate.doc: The maximum depth of the LALR(1) parser stack used to analyze input SQL statements.

to_generate.name: prepare_persistent
to_generate.doc: Hint to the query planner that the prepared statement will be retained for a long time and probably reused many times.

to_generate.name: prepare_normalize
to_generate.doc: A no-op flag previously required for using the sqlite3_normalized_sql() interface.

to_generate.name: prepare_no_vtab
to_generate.doc: Causes the SQL compiler to return an error if the statement uses any virtual tables.

to_generate.name: prepare_dont_log
to_generate.doc: Prevents SQL compiler errors from being sent to the error log defined by SQLITE_CONFIG_LOG.

to_generate.name: prepare_from_ddl
to_generate.doc: Enforces security constraints as if the SQL was derived from database schema DDL.

to_generate.name: soft_heap_limit64
to_generate.doc: Sets or queries SQLite's soft heap memory limit in bytes. Pass a negative value to query the current limit without changing it. Returns the previous limit, or a negative value on error. Setting to zero disables the heap limiter.
A handle to an open SQLite BLOB for incremental I/O.

to_generate.name: collation_needed
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked when an undefined collation sequence is needed for the database connection. The callback receives the database handle, the desired text encoding (as int), and the collation name (as String).

to_generate.name: overload_function
to_generate.doc: Ensures a global version of the given function name and arity exists so it can be overloaded by a virtual table. Creates a placeholder function if necessary.

to_generate.name: backup_pagecount
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of pages in the backup object referred to by the given handle. Returns -1 if the handle is invalid.

to_generate.name: wal_checkpoint
to_generate.doc: Checkpoints a database by transferring the WAL content for the database named 'db_name' on connection 'db' into the database file and resetting the log. Equivalent to sqlite3_wal_checkpoint_v2(db, db_name, SQLITE_CHECKPOINT_PASSIVE, 0, 0). Retained for backwards compatibility.

to_generate.name: exec
to_generate.doc: Executes one or more SQL statements in a single call. Optionally calls the callback for each result row. Returns a Dictionary with 'code' (SQLite result code) and 'error_message' (if any).

to_generate.name: wal_autocheckpoint
to_generate.doc: Configures automatic checkpointing for the given database connection. If N > 0, a checkpoint is triggered after every transaction that results in N or more frames in the WAL file. If N <= 0, auto-checkpointing is disabled. This replaces any existing WAL hook callback, and is itself disabled if a custom WAL hook is set.

to_generate.name: update_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers or unregisters a callback to be invoked whenever a row is updated, inserted, or deleted in a rowid table of the database. The callback receives four arguments: operation (one of SQLITE_INSERT, SQLITE_DELETE, or SQLITE_UPDATE), database name, table name, and rowid. Passing an invalid Callable removes the update hook.

to_generate.name: stricmp
to_generate.doc: Compares two UTF-8 strings in a case-insensitive manner, using SQLite's internal rules. Returns 0 if equal, <0 or >0 if not.

to_generate.name: prepare
to_generate.doc: Compiles an SQL statement into a byte-code program for execution. On success, out_stmt is set to the prepared statement. The unused portion of the SQL string is not returned by this wrapper. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: str_finish
to_generate.doc: Destroys the sqlite3_str object and returns the constructed string as a Godot String. Returns an empty string if errors were encountered or if the buffer is null.

to_generate.name: serialize_nocopy
to_generate.doc: Flags for sqlite3_serialize. SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY means that sqlite3_serialize() will return a pointer to the contiguous in-memory database that it is currently using, without making a copy of the database. If SQLite is not currently using a contiguous in-memory database, then this option causes sqlite3_serialize() to return a NULL pointer. SQLite will only be using a contiguous in-memory database if it has been initialized by a prior call to sqlite3_deserialize().
A wrapper class for SQLite's mutex methods structure, used to define custom mutex implementations for SQLite.

to_generate.name: vtab_constraint_support
to_generate.doc: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to declare virtual table constraint support. Pass as the second argument; if set to 0, constraints are not supported (the default), otherwise the virtual table promises safe constraint handling.

to_generate.name: vtab_innocuous
to_generate.doc: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to mark a virtual table as safe for use within triggers and views (innocuous).

to_generate.name: vtab_directonly
to_generate.doc: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to prohibit a virtual table from being used from within triggers and views.

to_generate.name: vtab_uses_all_schemas
to_generate.doc: Option value for sqlite3_vtab_config() to instruct the query planner to start at least a read transaction on all schemas whenever the virtual table is used.

to_generate.name: vtab_nochange
to_generate.doc: Determines if a virtual table column access is for an UPDATE where the column value will not change. Used within a virtual table's xColumn method for optimization.
A handle representing a snapshot of a SQLite WAL-mode database at a specific point in history.

to_generate.name: test_control
to_generate.doc: Testing and internal interface for SQLite. Allows reading and injecting state for SQLite testing purposes. The first parameter is an operation code; subsequent arguments vary by opcode. Only a limited subset of argument types are supported in this wrapper (int, String). Not intended for application use; interface is unstable and may change between releases.

to_generate.name: session_delete
to_generate.doc: Deletes a session object previously allocated using sqlite3session_create(). The session object becomes invalid after this call.

to_generate.name: value_text
to_generate.doc: Returns the UTF-8 string representation of the sqlite3_value, or an empty string if the value is not text.

to_generate.name: wal_checkpoint_v2
to_generate.doc: Runs a checkpoint operation on the specified database or all WAL databases attached to the given database connection. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (SQLite result code), 'n_log' (number of frames in the WAL log), and 'n_ckpt' (number of frames checkpointed).

to_generate.name: checkpoint_noop
to_generate.doc: Checkpoint mode: do no work at all.

to_generate.name: checkpoint_passive
to_generate.doc: Checkpoint mode: do as much as possible without blocking.

to_generate.name: checkpoint_full
to_generate.doc: Checkpoint mode: wait for writers, then checkpoint.

to_generate.name: checkpoint_restart
to_generate.doc: Checkpoint mode: like FULL but wait for readers.

to_generate.name: checkpoint_truncate
to_generate.doc: Checkpoint mode: like RESTART but also truncate WAL.

to_generate.name: deserialize_freeonclose
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize: SQLite should take ownership of the memory and free it when finished (call sqlite3_free() on close).

to_generate.name: deserialize_resizeable
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize: SQLite is allowed to grow the size of the database using sqlite3_realloc64().

to_generate.name: deserialize_readonly
to_generate.doc: Flag for sqlite3_deserialize: The deserialized database should be treated as read-only.
A wrapper class for the sqlite3_mutex handle.

to_generate.name: mutex_held
to_generate.body:
int result = ::sqlite3_mutex_held(handle.is_null() ? nullptr : handle->handle);
return result;
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the specified mutex is held by the calling thread. Intended for use in assertions only. Returns 1 if the mutex pointer is null.

to_generate.name: mutex_notheld
to_generate.body:
int result = ::sqlite3_mutex_notheld(handle.is_null() ? nullptr : handle->handle);
return result;
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the specified mutex is not held by the calling thread. Intended for use in assertions only. Returns 1 if the mutex pointer is null.
A wrapper class for the SQLite VFS (Virtual File System) object handle.

to_generate.name: file_control
to_generate.doc: Low-level control of database files. This method calls sqlite3_file_control(), allowing direct access to file control operations for the specified database. The db_name is the database name ("main", "temp", or as given in an ATTACH statement), or empty for the main database. The data parameter is passed as an integer value; only integer pointer opcodes are supported through this wrapper. The return value is the SQLite result code.

to_generate.name: autovacuum_pages
to_generate.doc: Registers or disables an autovacuum compaction callback for the database connection. The on_autovacuum Callable, if valid, will be invoked before each autovacuum operation. It receives (String schema_name, int n_db_page, int n_free_page, int n_byte_per_page) and must return the number of free pages to vacuum. If on_autovacuum is invalid, disables the callback. The on_userdata_destroy Callable is called when the callback is replaced or the database is closed, for user data cleanup.

to_generate.name: value_text16
to_generate.doc: Returns the value as a UTF-16 string. Returns an empty string if value is NULL.

to_generate.name: value_numeric_type
to_generate.doc: Returns the numeric type of the given SQLite value. The return value is one of the SQLite type codes, such as SQLITE_INTEGER or SQLITE_FLOAT.

to_generate.name: value_nochange
to_generate.doc: Returns non-zero if the value has not changed during an update or insert operation.

to_generate.name: value_text16le
to_generate.doc: Returns a pointer to the UTF-16LE encoded text value for the given sqlite3_value. The pointer is valid only until the next SQLite call and should not be freed or modified by the caller. Returns 0 if the value is not text or cannot be converted.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_lookaside_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of lookaside memory slots currently checked out.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_cache_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by all pager caches associated with the database connection.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_schema_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the schema for all databases associated with the connection.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_stmt_used
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap and lookaside memory used by all prepared statements associated with the database connection.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_lookaside_hit
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of malloc attempts satisfied using lookaside memory (high-water value only; current value is always zero).

to_generate.name: dbstatus_lookaside_miss_size
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of malloc attempts that failed due to requested size being larger than the lookaside slot size (high-water value only; current value is always zero).

to_generate.name: dbstatus_lookaside_miss_full
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of malloc attempts that failed due to all lookaside memory being in use (high-water value only; current value is always zero).

to_generate.name: dbstatus_cache_hit
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of pager cache hits that have occurred.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_cache_miss
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of pager cache misses that have occurred.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_cache_write
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of dirty cache entries that have been written to disk (excluding rollback/recovery writes). Overlaps with TEMPBUF_SPILL.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_deferred_fks
to_generate.doc: Returns zero if and only if all foreign key constraints have been resolved. High-water mark is always 0.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_cache_used_shared
to_generate.doc: Returns the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used by pager caches, dividing shared cache memory evenly among connections.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_cache_spill
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of dirty cache entries written to disk in the middle of a transaction due to cache overflow.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_tempbuf_spill
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of bytes written to temporary files on disk that could have been kept in memory had sufficient memory been available. Overlaps with CACHE_WRITE.

to_generate.name: dbstatus_max
to_generate.doc: Largest defined DBSTATUS verb value.

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_fast
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: fast.

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_recursive
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: recursive.

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_main
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static main.

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_mem
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static mem (used by sqlite3_malloc()).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_mem2
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static mem2 (not used).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_open
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static open (used by sqlite3BtreeOpen()).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_prng
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static prng (used by sqlite3_randomness()).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_lru
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static lru (lru page list).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_lru2
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static lru2 (not used).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_pmem
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static pmem (used by sqlite3PageMalloc()).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_app1
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static app1 (for use by application).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_app2
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static app2 (for use by application).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_app3
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static app3 (for use by application).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_vfs1
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static vfs1 (for use by built-in VFS).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_vfs2
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static vfs2 (for use by extension VFS).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_vfs3
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static vfs3 (for use by application VFS).

to_generate.name: sqlite_mutex_static_master
to_generate.doc: Mutex type: static master (legacy compatibility).

to_generate.name: value_bytes16
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of bytes in the value of this SQLite value object, interpreted as UTF-16 text (excluding null terminator).

to_generate.name: value_type
to_generate.doc: Returns the datatype code for the supplied sqlite3_value handle (e.g., SQLITE_INTEGER, SQLITE_TEXT, etc).
A wrapper class for the opaque sqlite3_value object from the SQLite C API.

to_generate.name: value_encoding
to_generate.doc: Returns the internal text encoding of the sqlite3_value object. Result is one of SQLITE_UTF8, SQLITE_UTF16BE, or SQLITE_UTF16LE if the value is of type TEXT; otherwise, the result is meaningless.

to_generate.name: value_frombind
to_generate.doc: Returns non-zero if the sqlite3_value object originated from a bind parameter.

to_generate.name: value_subtype
to_generate.doc: Returns the subtype for an application-defined SQL function argument value. Use this to pass context between SQL functions.

to_generate.name: value_dup
to_generate.doc: Creates a copy of the given sqlite3_value object. Returns a new handle, or null if the input is null, a pointer value, or on allocation failure.

to_generate.name: system_errno
to_generate.doc: Returns the underlying operating system error code that caused the most recent I/O error or failure to open a file on the given database handle. The return value is OS-dependent and may correspond to system-level error numbers such as errno on Unix.

to_generate.name: auto_extension
to_generate.doc: Registers a Godot Callable as a static SQLite extension entry point to be invoked on every new database connection. The Callable will receive (Ref<Sqlite3Handle> db, pointer as integer to error message, pointer as integer to sqlite3_api_routines). Multiple Callables can be registered; all will be invoked for each new connection.

to_generate.name: value_text16be
to_generate.doc: Returns the value as a String decoded from UTF-16BE. If the value is not text or cannot be converted, returns an empty String.

to_generate.name: sql
to_generate.doc: Returns the original SQL text used to create the prepared statement, or an empty string if unavailable. The returned string is managed by SQLite and is valid until the statement is finalized.

to_generate.name: normalized_sql
to_generate.doc: Returns the normalized SQL text for the prepared statement, or an empty string if not available. Only available if built with SQLITE_ENABLE_NORMALIZE.

to_generate.name: stmt_readonly
to_generate.doc: Returns true (non-zero) if the prepared statement makes no direct changes to the database file. See SQLite documentation for detailed caveats.

to_generate.name: vtab_rhs_value
to_generate.doc: Retrieves the right-hand operand value of a constraint in xBestIndex. Sets out_val to the value if available, or to null if not. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_NOTFOUND if the value is not available, or another error code.

to_generate.name: aggregate_context
to_generate.doc: Allocates or retrieves the per-aggregate state buffer for a custom SQL aggregate function. Returns the pointer to the state buffer as an integer, or 0 if allocation fails or n_bytes <= 0.

to_generate.name: serialize
to_generate.doc: Serializes the specified database into a PackedByteArray. The 'schema' argument specifies which schema to serialize (e.g., 'main', 'temp'). The 'flags' argument can be 0 or SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY. If SQLITE_SERIALIZE_NOCOPY is not set, the returned buffer is copied and freed after use; otherwise, only copied. Returns an empty array on error.
A wrapper class for the SQLite dynamically-typed value object (sqlite3_value), which can represent any SQLite value (integer, double, string, BLOB, or NULL).

to_generate.name: table_column_metadata
to_generate.doc: Extracts metadata about a column of a table. Returns a Dictionary with the following keys: "data_type" (String), "coll_seq" (String), "not_null" (int), "primary_key" (int), "autoinc" (int), and "error" (int SQLite status code). Returns SQLITE_OK (0) on success, or an error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: stmt_isexplain
to_generate.doc: Returns 1 if the prepared statement is an EXPLAIN statement, 2 if it is an EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN statement, or 0 if it is an ordinary statement or the handle is null.

to_generate.name: stmt_explain
to_generate.doc: Changes the EXPLAIN mode for a prepared statement. If e_mode is 0, the statement behaves normally; if 1, as if prefixed with EXPLAIN; if 2, as if prefixed with EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN. May fail if the statement cannot be reprepared or is active.

to_generate.name: stmt_busy
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the prepared statement has been stepped but not completed or reset. Returns false if the statement is null.

to_generate.name: bind_int
to_generate.doc: Binds an integer value to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

to_generate.name: progress_handler_disable
to_generate.doc: Disables the SQLite progress handler for the given database connection. Only passing null as the handler is supported in this wrapper due to C++ code generation constraints. This can be used to cancel any previously installed progress handler.

to_generate.name: bind_null
to_generate.doc: Binds a NULL value to a parameter in the prepared statement.

to_generate.name: bind_double
to_generate.doc: Binds a double value to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

to_generate.name: bind_zeroblob64
to_generate.doc: Binds a zero-filled BLOB of the specified size to the parameter at the given index in the prepared statement.

to_generate.name: clear_bindings
to_generate.doc: Resets all host parameter bindings on the prepared statement to NULL.

to_generate.name: bind_value
to_generate.doc: Binds a value to a parameter in a prepared statement.

to_generate.name: sqlite_deny
to_generate.doc: Abort the SQL statement with an error.

to_generate.name: sqlite_ignore
to_generate.doc: Don't allow access, but don't generate an error.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_index
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of an index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_table
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_temp_index
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a temporary index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.
