to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a temporary table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_temp_trigger
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a temporary trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_temp_view
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a temporary view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_trigger
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_view
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_delete
to_generate.doc: Authorize deletion from a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_index
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping an index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_table
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_temp_index
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a temporary index. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_temp_table
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a temporary table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_temp_trigger
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a temporary trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_temp_view
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a temporary view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_trigger
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a trigger. 3rd arg: Trigger Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_view
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a view. 3rd arg: View Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_insert
to_generate.doc: Authorize inserting into a table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_pragma
to_generate.doc: Authorize PRAGMA statement execution. 3rd arg: Pragma Name, 4th arg: 1st arg or NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_read
to_generate.doc: Authorize reading from a table column. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Column Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_select
to_generate.doc: Authorize SELECT statement. 3rd arg: NULL, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_transaction
to_generate.doc: Authorize transaction operations. 3rd arg: Operation, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_update
to_generate.doc: Authorize updating a table column. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Column Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_attach
to_generate.doc: Authorize ATTACH statement. 3rd arg: Filename, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_detach
to_generate.doc: Authorize DETACH statement. 3rd arg: Database Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_alter_table
to_generate.doc: Authorize ALTER TABLE statement. 3rd arg: Database Name, 4th arg: Table Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_reindex
to_generate.doc: Authorize REINDEX statement. 3rd arg: Index Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_analyze
to_generate.doc: Authorize ANALYZE statement. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_create_vtable
to_generate.doc: Authorize creation of a virtual table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Module Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_drop_vtable
to_generate.doc: Authorize dropping a virtual table. 3rd arg: Table Name, 4th arg: Module Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_function
to_generate.doc: Authorize use of a function. 3rd arg: NULL, 4th arg: Function Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_savepoint
to_generate.doc: Authorize SAVEPOINT operations. 3rd arg: Operation, 4th arg: Savepoint Name.

to_generate.name: sqlite_copy
to_generate.doc: No longer used. Deprecated.

to_generate.name: sqlite_recursive
to_generate.doc: Authorize recursive trigger or view. 3rd arg: NULL, 4th arg: NULL.

to_generate.name: bind_text
to_generate.doc: Binds a UTF-8 string value to a parameter in the prepared statement. The 'destructor_flag' should be -1 (SQLITE_TRANSIENT) to ensure SQLite copies the string, or 0 (SQLITE_STATIC) if the string buffer is guaranteed to remain valid during statement execution. Using SQLITE_TRANSIENT is recommended and safest when passing Godot strings.

to_generate.name: column_origin_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the origin name of the column for the result set of a prepared statement, or an empty string if not available.

to_generate.name: column_database_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the name of the database from which the Nth result column of the prepared statement was extracted, or an empty string if not applicable.

to_generate.name: bind_text16
to_generate.doc: Binds a UTF-16 encoded string value to the prepared statement at the given parameter index. The value is copied by SQLite (SQLITE_TRANSIENT). Pass -1 for length to use null-terminated string.

to_generate.name: bind_parameter_index
to_generate.doc: Returns the index of an SQL parameter with the given name for the prepared statement. Returns zero if no matching parameter is found. The name must be given in UTF-8.

to_generate.name: bind_parameter_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the name of the N-th SQL parameter in the prepared statement, including any prefix (':', '$', '@', or '?'). Returns an empty string if the parameter is nameless or out of range.

to_generate.name: column_text
to_generate.doc: Returns the value of the column as a UTF-8 string. Returns an empty string if the value is NULL.

to_generate.name: sqlite_integer
to_generate.doc: Code for the SQLite INTEGER datatype.

to_generate.name: sqlite_float
to_generate.doc: Code for the SQLite FLOAT datatype.

to_generate.name: sqlite_blob
to_generate.doc: Code for the SQLite BLOB datatype.

to_generate.name: sqlite_null
to_generate.doc: Code for the SQLite NULL datatype.

to_generate.name: bind_parameter_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of SQL parameters in the given prepared statement. SQL parameters are tokens such as '?', '?NNN', ':AAA', '$AAA', or '@AAA' that act as placeholders for bound values.

to_generate.name: data_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of columns in the current row of the result set of the prepared statement. Returns 0 if the statement does not have results ready, is null, or has completed execution.

to_generate.name: bind_text64
to_generate.doc: Binds a string parameter to an SQL statement, specifying the number of bytes and encoding. You may control the destructor callback using standard SQLite macros or values.

to_generate.name: column_decltype
to_generate.doc: Returns the declared type of the Nth column of the result set of a SELECT statement, or an empty string if the column is an expression or subquery. The returned string is UTF-8 encoded.

to_generate.name: column_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of columns in the result set of the given prepared statement. Returns 0 if the statement returns no data (e.g., for an UPDATE).

to_generate.name: column_table_name16
to_generate.doc: Returns the name of the table for the specified result column of a prepared statement in UTF-16 encoding. Returns an empty string if not applicable.

to_generate.name: column_origin_name16
to_generate.doc: Returns the origin column name for the given column in the result set as a UTF-16 string, or an empty string if not available.

to_generate.name: column_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the name assigned to the specified column in the result set of a SELECT statement. Returns an empty string on error.

to_generate.name: prepare_v2
to_generate.doc: Prepares an SQL statement for execution. Returns a dictionary with 'error_code' (int), 'statement' (Ref<Sqlite3StmtHandle>), and 'tail' (String with unused SQL).

to_generate.name: prepare16_v2
to_generate.doc: Prepares a UTF-16 SQL statement for execution. On success, sets out_stmt to a prepared statement handle. Returns a SQLite error code.

to_generate.name: step
to_generate.doc: Evaluates a prepared SQLite statement. Returns an SQLite result code (e.g. SQLITE_ROW, SQLITE_DONE, SQLITE_ERROR, etc.).

to_generate.name: prepare_v3
to_generate.doc: Compiles a SQL statement into a prepared statement object using sqlite3_prepare_v3. On success, sets out_stmt to the created statement handle. Returns a SQLite error code. The unused portion of the SQL string is not returned.

to_generate.name: column_value
to_generate.doc: Returns the value of the column in the current row of the result set for the given prepared statement.

to_generate.name: prepare16_v3
to_generate.doc: Compiles a SQL statement (UTF-16 encoded). Returns a Dictionary with the SQLite result code as 'result', the compiled statement as 'stmt', and the offset in bytes of the unused portion of the SQL string as 'tail_offset'.

to_generate.name: prepare16
to_generate.doc: Compiles a UTF-16 encoded SQL statement into a prepared statement object. Returns the SQLite result code. The unused SQL tail is not accessible from this wrapper.

to_generate.name: column_bytes
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of bytes in the value of the specified column in the current row of the result set of a prepared statement.

to_generate.name: column_type
to_generate.doc: Returns the data type code for the value of the i_col-th column in the current row of the result set for the given prepared statement.

to_generate.name: column_text16
to_generate.doc: Returns the value of the specified column as a UTF-16 encoded string, or an empty string if the value is NULL.

to_generate.name: finalize
to_generate.doc: Destroys a prepared statement object and releases associated resources. If the statement was never evaluated or the last evaluation was successful, returns SQLITE_OK. If the last evaluation failed, returns the appropriate error code. Invoking on a NULL pointer is a harmless no-op. After finalization, the statement must not be used.

to_generate.name: column_bytes16
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of bytes in the value of the specified column as UTF-16 for the current row of the statement.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstatus_reset
to_generate.doc: Zero all sqlite3_stmt_scanstatus() related event counters for the given statement. Only available if built with SQLITE_ENABLE_STMT_SCANSTATUS.

to_generate.name: reset
to_generate.doc: Resets the prepared statement to its initial state, ready to be re-executed. Bound variable values are retained. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code on failure. Bindings are not cleared.

to_generate.name: db_name
to_generate.doc: Returns the schema name (as a String) for the N-th database on the connection. The returned string is managed by SQLite and may be invalidated after schema changes; copy if needed for long-term use.

to_generate.name: create_collation16
to_generate.doc: Registers a new collation sequence with the database. The collation name must be a UTF-16 string. The x_compare callable receives three arguments: user_data (as passed), the first string, and the second string (both as Godot String). The callable must return an integer indicating the sort order.

to_generate.name: result_error_code
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of the context to an error code.

to_generate.name: db_cacheflush
to_generate.doc: Flushes dirty pages in the pager cache of the specified database connection to disk for all schemas (main, temp, and attached databases). Returns SQLITE_OK on success, SQLITE_BUSY if locks cannot be obtained, or another SQLite error code on failure. Does not modify the database handle error state.

to_generate.name: bind_pointer
to_generate.doc: Binds an opaque pointer parameter to a prepared SQLite statement at the given index. The 'pointer' argument should be an integer representing a native pointer (void*). The 'type' describes the pointer type as a UTF-8 string. Custom destructor callbacks are not supported for safety; the pointer will not be automatically freed by SQLite.

to_generate.name: result_int64
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of the current SQLite function context to the given 64-bit integer value.

to_generate.name: context_db_handle
to_generate.doc: Returns a handle to the database connection associated with the given function context.

to_generate.name: user_data
to_generate.doc: Returns the user data pointer (as an integer) associated with the given SQLite user function context. This corresponds to the pUserData parameter passed when the function was registered. Must be called from the same thread in which the application-defined function is running.

to_generate.name: sqlite_carray_int32
to_generate.doc: Data is 32-bit signed integers.

to_generate.name: sqlite_carray_int64
to_generate.doc: Data is 64-bit signed integers.

to_generate.name: sqlite_carray_double
to_generate.doc: Data is doubles.

to_generate.name: sqlite_carray_text
to_generate.doc: Data is char*.

to_generate.name: sqlite_carray_blob
to_generate.doc: Data is struct iovec.

to_generate.name: carray_int32
to_generate.doc: Data is 32-bit signed integers.

to_generate.name: carray_int64
to_generate.doc: Data is 64-bit signed integers.

to_generate.name: carray_double
to_generate.doc: Data is doubles.

to_generate.name: carray_text
to_generate.doc: Data is char*.

to_generate.name: carray_blob
to_generate.doc: Data is struct iovec.

to_generate.name: get_auxdata
to_generate.doc: Returns a pointer to the auxiliary data associated with the Nth argument value of the current SQL function context. Returns 0 if there is no auxiliary data.

to_generate.name: result_null
to_generate.doc: Sets the result for the current SQLite function context to NULL.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_fullscan_step
to_generate.doc: This is the number of times that SQLite has stepped forward in a table as part of a full table scan.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_sort
to_generate.doc: This is the number of sort operations that have occurred.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_autoindex
to_generate.doc: This is the number of rows inserted into transient indices that were created automatically in order to help joins run faster.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_vm_step
to_generate.doc: This is the number of virtual machine operations executed by the prepared statement if that number is less than or equal to 2147483647.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_reprepare
to_generate.doc: This is the number of times that the prepared statement has been automatically regenerated due to schema changes or changes to bound parameters that might affect the query plan.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_run
to_generate.doc: This is the number of times that the prepared statement has been run.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_filter_miss
to_generate.doc: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_MISS is the number of times that a join step was processed as normal because a Bloom filter returned a find.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_filter_hit
to_generate.doc: SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FILTER_HIT is the number of times that a join step was bypassed because a Bloom filter returned not-found.

to_generate.name: stmtstatus_memused
to_generate.doc: This is the approximate number of bytes of heap memory used to store the prepared statement.

to_generate.name: commit_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked whenever a transaction is committed on the specified SQLite database. The callback must return an integer: return 0 to allow the commit or nonzero to convert the commit into a rollback. Passing a null Callable disables the hook. Returns the previous user data pointer as an integer.

to_generate.name: result_int
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQL function to the specified integer value.

to_generate.name: carray_bind
to_generate.doc: Binds an array of data to the specified parameter of the prepared statement. The data is passed as a PackedByteArray (raw memory). This version always uses SQLITE_TRANSIENT for the destructor to ensure memory safety. Returns the SQLite status code.

to_generate.name: get_clientdata
to_generate.doc: Returns the raw pointer associated with the given name for the database connection, as previously set by sqlite3_set_clientdata. Returns 0 if not set. This function is intended for advanced use, returns an unsafe pointer as an integer, and should not be exposed to scripts. Security warning: improper use can compromise process safety.

to_generate.name: result_zeroblob
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of the context to a BLOB of length n, filled with zero bytes.

to_generate.name: result_subtype
to_generate.doc: Sets the subtype of the result for an application-defined SQL function using the given context. Only the lower 8 bits of the subtype are preserved in current versions of SQLite.

to_generate.name: result_error
to_generate.doc: Sets an error result for a user-defined SQL function. The error message is passed as a UTF-8 string and the length in bytes.

to_generate.name: aggregate_count
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Returns the aggregate count for the given context. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

to_generate.name: expired
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Checks if the given statement is expired. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

to_generate.name: transfer_bindings
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Transfers bindings from one statement to another. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

to_generate.name: global_recover
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Recovers from a global error state. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

to_generate.name: thread_cleanup
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Cleans up thread resources. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

to_generate.name: memory_alarm
to_generate.doc: DEPRECATED. Sets a memory alarm with a callback. This function is deprecated and should be avoided in new code.

to_generate.name: result_text64
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQL function to a string value, using the specified encoding and length. The destructor is always SQLITE_TRANSIENT (data is copied by SQLite).
A wrapper class for the sqlite3_context handle, used for SQLite user-defined function contexts.

to_generate.name: result_error_toobig
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of a user-defined SQLite function to an error indicating that the result is too big.

to_generate.name: sqlite_utf8
to_generate.doc: Text is encoding as UTF-8.

to_generate.name: sqlite_utf16le
to_generate.doc: Text is encoding as UTF-16 with each code point being expressed little endian (least significant byte first).

to_generate.name: sqlite_utf16be
to_generate.doc: Text is encoding as UTF-16 with each code point being expressed big endian (most significant byte first).

to_generate.name: sqlite_utf16
to_generate.doc: Text is encoding as UTF-16 with code points expressed in native byte order.

to_generate.name: sqlite_any
to_generate.doc: Represents no preference for the text encoding; deprecated.

to_generate.name: sqlite_utf16_aligned
to_generate.doc: Input strings are UTF-16 in native byte order and aligned to a 2-byte boundary.

to_generate.name: sqlite_utf8_zt
to_generate.doc: Zero-terminated UTF-8 encoding for certain SQLite APIs.

to_generate.name: sqlite_deterministic
to_generate.doc: The function always gives the same output with the same input parameters.

to_generate.name: sqlite_directonly
to_generate.doc: The function may only be invoked from top-level SQL, not inside views, triggers, or schema structures.

to_generate.name: sqlite_subtype
to_generate.doc: The function might call sqlite3_value_subtype() to inspect argument sub-types.

to_generate.name: sqlite_innocuous
to_generate.doc: The function is unlikely to cause problems even if misused; no side effects.

to_generate.name: sqlite_result_subtype
to_generate.doc: The function might call sqlite3_result_subtype() to associate a sub-type with its result.

to_generate.name: sqlite_selforder1
to_generate.doc: Indicates the function is an aggregate that internally orders values of its first argument.
Obsolete SQLite pcache_methods handle, retained for backwards compatibility.

to_generate.name: result_zeroblob64
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of the SQL function to a zero-filled BLOB of the specified size in bytes.

to_generate.name: result_text16le
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQL function to be the UTF-16LE encoded text provided in 'data'. The destructor parameter controls memory management: pass -1 for SQLITE_TRANSIENT (SQLite will make its own copy), 0 for SQLITE_STATIC (no copy, do not free), or a function pointer (as int64_t) for custom cleanup.

to_generate.name: result_text
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQL function (within a UDF) to a UTF-8 text string. 'context' is the function context. 'text' is the string result. 'length' is the number of bytes to use from 'text' (or negative for null-terminated). 'destructor' is a pointer specifying how the library should manage the memory: pass SQLITE_TRANSIENT or SQLITE_STATIC, typically obtained via the corresponding wrapper functions.

to_generate.name: result_pointer
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of a user-defined SQLite function to a pointer value, associated with a type string, and optionally a destructor callback.
A wrapper for the SQLite virtual table module structure (sqlite3_module), which defines the callback methods for a virtual table implementation.

to_generate.name: str_errcode
to_generate.doc: Returns the error code for the given sqlite3_str object: SQLITE_OK for no error, SQLITE_NOMEM for out-of-memory, or SQLITE_TOOBIG if too large.

to_generate.name: create_function_v2
to_generate.doc: Registers a new SQL function or aggregate in the SQLite database. The Godot Callables x_func, x_step, and x_final correspond to the scalar function, aggregate step, and aggregate finalizer respectively. Each Callable is optional; pass an invalid Callable for unused callbacks. The Callables receive (context: Ref<Sqlite3ContextHandle>, args: Array of Ref<Sqlite3ValueHandle>).
A wrapper class for the SQLite online backup object.

to_generate.name: create_window_function
to_generate.doc: Registers a new window function with SQLite. The provided Callables are used as callbacks: 'step' (sqlite3_context, argc, argv), 'final' (sqlite3_context), 'value' (sqlite3_context), 'inverse' (sqlite3_context, argc, argv), and 'destroy' (no args). Each callback receives Godot handle wrappers for the C context and value pointers. Returns an SQLite error code.

to_generate.name: vtab_collation
to_generate.doc: Returns the collation name for the given constraint in a virtual table's xBestIndex() context. If the constraint uses a COLLATE operator, its name is returned; otherwise, the column's COLLATE clause or 'BINARY' is returned. Only call this during xBestIndex.
A wrapper class for the SQLite custom page cache methods structure.

to_generate.name: blob_reopen
to_generate.doc: Moves the blob handle to point to a different row within the same table. The new row is identified by rowid. If the row is invalid, the handle is aborted and cannot be used for further operations.

to_generate.name: blob_bytes
to_generate.doc: Returns the size in bytes of the BLOB accessible via the given open BLOB handle.

to_generate.name: result_text16be
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQLite user function to the given UTF-16BE encoded text. The 'text' argument must be a PackedByteArray containing UTF-16BE bytes. The 'length' argument specifies how many bytes to use from 'text'. The 'destructor' argument should be SQLITE_TRANSIENT or SQLITE_STATIC (as integer constants), or a pointer value for an appropriate custom destructor function, if needed.
Opaque handle for a SQLite3 page cache object.
Opaque handle for a SQLite3 page cache page.
Page cache methods interface for SQLite3, allowing custom page cache implementations.

to_generate.name: snapshot_cmp
to_generate.doc: Compares the ages of two valid snapshot handles. Returns a negative value if p1 is older than p2, zero if they refer to the same snapshot, and a positive value if p1 is newer. The comparison is undefined if the snapshots are not from the same database or were obtained before the last WAL file reset.

to_generate.name: status
to_generate.doc: Retrieves runtime status information about the performance of SQLite. Returns a Dictionary containing the status code (as 'result'), the current value (as 'current'), and the highest recorded value (as 'highwater'). The meaning and validity of 'current' and 'highwater' depend on the specific status parameter passed as 'op'. If 'reset_flag' is true, the highwater mark may be reset after reading.

to_generate.name: blob_close
to_generate.doc: Closes an open BLOB handle. The handle is set to null after closing. Returns the SQLite error code (0 for SQLITE_OK). Passing a null or invalid handle is a no-op.

to_generate.name: snapshot_recover
to_generate.doc: Scans the WAL file for the specified database and makes all valid snapshots available to sqlite3_snapshot_open(). Only available if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT. Returns SQLITE_OK if successful, or an SQLite error code otherwise.

to_generate.name: session_objconfig_size
to_generate.doc: Option flag for enabling or disabling the sqlite3session_changeset_size() API.

to_generate.name: session_objconfig_rowid
to_generate.doc: Option flag for enabling or disabling collection of data for tables with no explicit PRIMARY KEY.

to_generate.name: snapshot_open
to_generate.doc: Starts a read transaction or upgrades an existing one on the given schema of the specified database connection so that it refers to the historical snapshot. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an appropriate error code. The database must not be in autocommit mode and both the schema and snapshot must be valid. This function is only available when compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT.

to_generate.name: create_collation
to_generate.doc: Adds or modifies a collation associated with the database connection. The compare Callable should take arguments (user_data, PackedByteArray a, PackedByteArray b) and return int. Note: The user_data and Callable are kept alive for the lifetime of the collation; memory is not automatically freed by SQLite. Removing the collation leaks the context unless managed by the user.

to_generate.name: blob_write
to_generate.doc: Writes data from the provided buffer into an open BLOB handle, starting at the specified offset. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an SQLite error code on failure. The BLOB must be opened for writing. The buffer size determines the number of bytes written.

to_generate.name: session_object_config
to_generate.doc: This method is used to configure a session object after it has been created. At present the only valid values for the second parameter are SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE and SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_ROWID.
Opaque handle for an ongoing SQLite online backup operation.

to_generate.name: backup_init
to_generate.doc: Initializes an online backup operation from the source database to the destination database. Returns a backup handle on success, or null on failure.

to_generate.name: session_indirect
to_generate.doc: Sets, clears, or queries the indirect change flag for a session object. Pass 0 to clear, greater than 0 to set, or less than 0 to query the current state. Returns 0 if the flag is clear, or 1 if set.

to_generate.name: result_text16
to_generate.doc: Sets the result of an SQL function to the given UTF-16 encoded text. The destructor argument should be SQLITE_TRANSIENT or SQLITE_STATIC. The length parameter specifies the number of bytes, or -1 for null-terminated input.

to_generate.name: session_attach
to_generate.doc: Attaches a table to the session for change tracking. If the table name is empty, all tables in the database are tracked. Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an SQLite error code on failure.
A wrapper class for the SQLite virtual file system (VFS) object.

to_generate.name: vfs_find
to_generate.doc: Finds and returns a SQLite virtual file system (VFS) object by its name. Names are case sensitive and must be provided as UTF-8 strings. If no match is found, or if the name is empty, the default VFS is returned. Returns an empty reference if no VFS is found.

to_generate.name: changeset_size
to_generate.doc: Returns an upper limit (in bytes) for the size of the changeset that might be produced if changeset() is called on this session. Returns 0 unless the session was configured with SQLITE_SESSION_OBJCONFIG_SIZE.

to_generate.name: changesetstart_invert
to_generate.doc: Invert the changeset while iterating through it. This is equivalent to inverting a changeset using sqlite3changeset_invert() before applying it. It is an error to specify this flag with a patchset.

to_generate.name: session_table_filter
to_generate.doc: Sets a table filter on a SQLite3 Session object. The filter Callable is invoked for each table not yet attached to the session to determine whether changes to the table should be tracked. The Callable receives the table name as a String and must return an int; returning 0 disables tracking for that table. Note: The filter Callable and context may remain in memory for the session's lifetime.

to_generate.name: is_empty
to_generate.doc: Returns non-zero if no changes to attached tables have been recorded by the session object. Returns zero if one or more changes have been recorded. Note: Even if this returns zero, the changeset may still contain no changes if modifications were undone.
A wrapper class for a sqlite3 changeset iterator.

to_generate.name: finalize
to_generate.doc: Finalizes a changeset iterator created with sqlite3changeset_start(). Returns SQLITE_OK on success or an error code if an error occurred. Passing an invalid or misused iterator returns SQLITE_MISUSE.

to_generate.name: stmt_scanstatus_v2
to_generate.doc: Retrieves scan status information for a prepared SQLite3 statement. Returns a Dictionary with 'status' (int) and 'value' (Variant). The type of 'value' depends on scan_status_op: int for most values, double for 2 (EST), and String for 3 (NAME). See SQLite documentation for details.
A wrapper class for the sqlite3 changeset iterator handle.

to_generate.name: changeset_start
to_generate.doc: Creates an iterator used to traverse a changeset. On success, out_iter will be set to a new iterator handle and SQLITE_OK is returned. On error, out_iter is not modified and an SQLite error code is returned. The changeset_blob must remain valid until the iterator is destroyed.

to_generate.name: changeset_old
to_generate.doc: Obtains the original value of a column from a changeset iterator for DELETE or UPDATE changes. On success, sets out_value to a handle for the old column value and returns SQLITE_OK. Returns an error code and sets out_value to null if not successful.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict
to_generate.doc: Obtains the i_val'th value from the conflicting row in a changeset iterator during a conflict-handler callback. Sets out_value on success. Only valid in specific callback contexts. Returns the SQLite result code.

to_generate.name: rebase
to_generate.doc: Rebases a changeset using the specified rebaser configuration. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (the SQLite result code) and 'buffer' (the rebased changeset as a PackedByteArray, or empty on error).
A wrapper class for the legacy FTS5 tokenizer handle.

to_generate.name: blob_read
to_generate.doc: Reads N bytes from the open BLOB handle starting at the given offset and returns them as a PackedByteArray. On error, returns an empty array.

to_generate.name: session_changeset
to_generate.doc: Generates a changeset from the session object. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int SQLite error code) and 'changeset' (PackedByteArray of the changeset data or empty on error).

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_strm
to_generate.doc: Applies a streaming changeset to a SQLite database. The input, filter, and conflict callbacks are provided as Godot Callables. The x_input Callable will be called with (input_ctx, PackedByteArray buffer) and must return a PackedByteArray containing the next chunk of data. The x_filter Callable will be called with (conflict_ctx, String table_name) and must return int; if not provided, all tables are included. The x_conflict Callable will be called with (conflict_ctx, int eConflict, Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle iter) and must return int. Returns the SQLite error code.

to_generate.name: create_function
to_generate.doc: Creates or redefines an SQL function or aggregate for the given SQLite database connection. The provided Callables are stored for use by user-supplied trampolines, but by default no trampolines are provided (all callbacks are null). This allows deletion of an existing SQL function or aggregate, but to register a new Godot-based implementation, the user must supply appropriate C trampoline functions elsewhere. The function name must not exceed 255 UTF-8 bytes. The app_data is stored in the context for callbacks. Returns the SQLite result code.

to_generate.name: session_patchset
to_generate.doc: Generates a patchset blob from a session object. Returns a Dictionary with the SQLite result code as 'code' and the patchset data as 'patchset' (PackedByteArray).

to_generate.name: changesetapply_nosavepoint_flag
to_generate.doc: Flag: Omit savepoint when applying a changeset.

to_generate.name: changesetapply_invert_flag
to_generate.doc: Flag: Invert the changeset before applying.

to_generate.name: changesetapply_ignorenoop_flag
to_generate.doc: Flag: Ignore no-op changes when applying a changeset.

to_generate.name: changesetapply_fknoaction_flag
to_generate.doc: Flag: Foreign key constraints behave as NO ACTION.

to_generate.name: changeset_data_type
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler type: Data conflict.

to_generate.name: changeset_notfound_type
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler type: Row not found.

to_generate.name: changeset_conflict_type
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler type: Primary key conflict.

to_generate.name: changeset_constraint_type
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler type: Constraint violation.

to_generate.name: changeset_foreign_key_type
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler type: Foreign key violation.

to_generate.name: changeset_omit_result
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler result: Omit the change.

to_generate.name: changeset_replace_result
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler result: Replace the conflicting row.

to_generate.name: changeset_abort_result
to_generate.doc: Conflict handler result: Abort and roll back changes.

to_generate.name: changegroup_schema
to_generate.doc: Enforces that changesets added to the changegroup must match the schema of the specified database. Returns SQLITE_SCHEMA if the changeset is not compatible; otherwise returns an SQLite status code.

to_generate.name: changeset_invert
to_generate.doc: Inverts a changeset buffer so that applying the inverted changeset reverses the effects of the original. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (the SQLite error code) and 'inverted' (the inverted changeset as a PackedByteArray, or empty if an error occurred).

to_generate.name: changegroup_add
to_generate.doc: Adds all changes within the changeset or patchset to the changegroup. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code on failure. The changeset data is provided as a PackedByteArray.

to_generate.name: tokenize_query
to_generate.doc: Flag for xTokenize(): Query mode.

to_generate.name: tokenize_prefix
to_generate.doc: Flag for xTokenize(): Prefix mode.

to_generate.name: tokenize_document
to_generate.doc: Flag for xTokenize(): Document mode.

to_generate.name: tokenize_aux
to_generate.doc: Flag for xTokenize(): Auxiliary mode.

to_generate.name: token_colocated
to_generate.doc: Flag for xToken callback: Same position as previous token.

to_generate.name: changegroup_output_strm
to_generate.doc: Calls the provided Callable for each data chunk output from the changegroup. The Callable receives a PackedByteArray with the data and must return an int status. Returning non-zero from the callback will stop output and the function returns that value.

to_generate.name: add_change
to_generate.doc: Adds the single change currently indicated by the iterator to the changegroup object. The rules for adding the change are as described for sqlite3changegroup_add(). If the change is successfully added to the changegroup, SQLITE_OK is returned. Otherwise, an SQLite error code is returned. The iterator must point to a valid entry when this function is called. If it does not, SQLITE_ERROR is returned and no change is added to the changegroup. Additionally, the iterator must not have been opened with the SQLITE_CHANGESETAPPLY_INVERT flag; in this case, SQLITE_ERROR is also returned.

to_generate.name: pk
to_generate.doc: Obtains the primary key column definition for the current table in the changeset iterator. Returns a dictionary with the result code, a byte array where each entry is 1 if the corresponding column is part of the primary key, and the number of columns.

to_generate.name: changeset_start_strm
to_generate.doc: Begins a changeset iterator using a streaming input callback. The x_input Callable is called with the maximum number of bytes to read and must return a PackedByteArray of data. The resulting iterator handle is set into out_iter. Returns SQLITE_OK on success, or an error code on failure.

to_generate.name: changeset_invert_strm
to_generate.doc: Inverts a changeset stream using provided Godot Callables for input and output. The `x_input` Callable should accept an integer (max bytes to read) and return a PackedByteArray containing the data read (up to that size). The `x_output` Callable receives a PackedByteArray (data to write) and should return an int (result code). Returning a non-zero value from either callback may indicate an error and stop processing.

to_generate.name: mutex_alloc
to_generate.doc: Allocates a new SQLite mutex or returns a static mutex pointer depending on the mutex type. Returns null if unable to allocate.

to_generate.name: preupdate_hook
to_generate.doc: Registers a preupdate hook callback to be invoked prior to each INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operation. Pass an invalid Callable to unregister. Only available if SQLite is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK.

to_generate.name: preupdate_old
to_generate.doc: Writes into out_value a protected sqlite3_value containing the old value of the Nth column before update/delete. Only valid within preupdate callback for UPDATE or DELETE.

to_generate.name: preupdate_count
to_generate.doc: Returns the number of columns in the row being inserted, updated, or deleted during a preupdate event.

to_generate.name: preupdate_depth
to_generate.doc: Returns the trigger recursion depth at which the preupdate callback was invoked.

to_generate.name: preupdate_new
to_generate.doc: Writes into out_value a protected sqlite3_value containing the new value of the Nth column after insert/update. Only valid within preupdate callback for INSERT or UPDATE.

to_generate.name: preupdate_blobwrite
to_generate.doc: Returns the index of the column being written during a blob write in a preupdate event, or -1 otherwise.

to_generate.name: str_appendf
to_generate.doc: Appends formatted text to the sqlite3_str object using Godot-style formatting (`{0}`, `{1}`, ...). The format and arguments are specified as a Godot format string and an array of arguments. Internally, this formats the string in Godot and appends it using sqlite3_str_appendall. Note: SQLite's printf-style formatting is NOT supported through this wrapper.

to_generate.name: session_diff
to_generate.doc: Loads the difference between tables into a session. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'result' (SQLite error code) and 'error_message' (String with error details, if any).

to_generate.name: create_function16
to_generate.doc: Registers a new user-defined SQL function (scalar or aggregate) with the SQLite database using a UTF-16 function name. The Godot Callables are invoked as follows: scalar function: x_func(ctx_handle, [value_handle1, ...]); aggregate step: x_step(ctx_handle, [value_handle1, ...]); aggregate final: x_final(ctx_handle). All Callable arguments are Godot handle wrappers for the underlying SQLite pointers. The user is responsible for calling the appropriate sqlite3_result_* method on the context handle inside x_func or x_final. Returns the SQLite error code. The context struct's memory is not freed until the function is replaced or the database is closed.

to_generate.name: changeset_op
to_generate.doc: Obtain information about the current operation from a changeset iterator. Returns a Dictionary containing the error code and, if successful, the table name, column count, operation type, and optionally whether the change is indirect.

to_generate.name: changeset_new
to_generate.doc: Obtains the 'new' value for a given column from the changeset iterator. This is only valid for UPDATE or INSERT changes. On success, sets out_value to the new value or NULL.

to_generate.name: fk_conflicts
to_generate.doc: Returns a Dictionary with the integer fields 'result' (the function status code) and 'fk_violations' (the number of known foreign key violations in the destination database for the given changeset iterator). Should only be called from within an SQLITE_CHANGESET_FOREIGN_KEY conflict handler callback. On success, 'result' is SQLITE_OK; otherwise, SQLITE_MISUSE.

to_generate.name: changegroup_output
to_generate.doc: Obtain a buffer containing a changeset (or patchset) representing the current contents of the changegroup. Returns the binary buffer as a PackedByteArray on success, or an empty array on error. The buffer is managed internally and freed after copying.
Opaque handle for iterating over changesets in SQLite.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply
to_generate.doc: Apply a changeset or patchset to a database. All changes are enclosed in a savepoint transaction. The filter callback, if given, is called once for each table affected; returning zero skips changes for that table. The conflict callback is invoked for each conflict encountered during apply. Returns an SQLite result code.

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v3
to_generate.doc: Applies a changeset to the database, invoking x_filter for each table and x_conflict for each conflict encountered. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (SQLite result code) and 'rebase' (PackedByteArray of rebase data, if produced).

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v2
to_generate.doc: Applies a changeset to the main database handle. Calls `x_filter(String table_name)` for each table, and `x_conflict(int64_t conflict_type, Ref<Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle> iter)` on conflict. Returns a Dictionary with keys: 'result' (the SQLite result code) and 'rebase' (PackedByteArray of rebase data, if any).

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v2_strm
to_generate.doc: Applies a changeset to the given SQLite database handle using streaming input. Callbacks are provided for input, table filtering, and conflict resolution. Returns a Dictionary with keys 'error' (int, the result code) and 'rebase' (PackedByteArray, the rebase data if generated, or empty if not).

to_generate.name: rebase_strm
to_generate.doc: Streams rebasing data using the provided input and output callbacks. The x_input callback is called with the requested buffer size (int), and must return a PackedByteArray with the data to read. The x_output callback is called with a PackedByteArray containing the data to write, and must return an int status. Returning nonzero from either callback signals an error.

to_generate.name: session_config
to_generate.doc: Configures global parameters for the SQLite sessions module. This function is not threadsafe and must be called before any session-related objects are created. The 'chunk_size' argument is both input and output: pass the desired chunk size (in bytes), and on return it will be set to the final value used by the streaming interface. Returns a Dictionary with 'result' (the status code) and 'chunk_size' (the resulting chunk size).

to_generate.name: changeset_apply_v3_strm
to_generate.doc:
Applies a changeset to the database using streaming callbacks.

Arguments:
- db: The SQLite database handle (must be valid).
- x_input: Callable for reading changeset data. Receives one argument (buffer_size:int). Return a Dictionary {buffer: PackedByteArray, result: int}, where buffer contains the bytes read and result is 0 for success or non-zero for error/EOF.
- x_filter: Callable for filtering tables. Receives one argument (Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle). Return int (non-zero to include, zero to exclude).
- x_conflict: Callable for conflict resolution. Receives two arguments: (int eConflict, Sqlite3ChangesetIterHandle). Return int (resolution action).
- flags: Apply flags.

Returns: Dictionary with keys:
- 'result': The SQLite result code from the operation.
- 'rebase': (PackedByteArray) If rebase data was produced, contains the rebase as a byte array. Otherwise null.
A wrapper class for sqlite3_index_info, used in the virtual table interface for SQLite to communicate index selection information.

to_generate.name: drop_modules
to_generate.doc: Removes all virtual table modules from the SQLite connection except for those named in keep_list. If keep_list is empty, all modules are removed.

to_generate.name: create_module_v2
to_generate.doc: Registers a virtual table module with the SQLite database connection. The module name (z_name) and method table (module) are required. client_data is passed to xCreate/xConnect and will be provided to on_destroy (if supplied) when the module is destroyed. If on_destroy is provided, it will be called with client_data as its argument when the module is unregistered.

to_generate.name: declare_vtab
to_generate.doc: Declares the schema of a virtual table. Used by xCreate and xConnect methods of a virtual table module to specify the format (column names and types) of the virtual table via a CREATE TABLE SQL statement.

to_generate.name: vtab_distinct
to_generate.doc: Returns an integer (0-3) describing the DISTINCT/ordering requirements for a virtual table query, as determined by SQLite's query planner. This API should only be called from within a virtual table's xBestIndex method. The return value indicates how the output should be ordered or deduplicated: 0 = rows in orderBy order, 1 = group-adjacent rows, 2 = group-adjacent rows with optional duplicate omission, 3 = ordered with optional duplicate omission.

to_generate.name: vtab_in
to_generate.doc: Identify and handle IN constraints in xBestIndex. Used by virtual table implementations during xBestIndex to query or specify whether a constraint is an IN operator and whether it should be processed all at once or individually.

to_generate.name: vtab_in_first
to_generate.doc: Finds the first element on the right-hand side of an IN constraint for a virtual table xFilter parameter. On success, sets out_val to the first value and returns SQLITE_OK. If there are no more values, sets out_val to null and returns SQLITE_DONE. Returns an error code on failure.

to_generate.name: unlock_notify
to_generate.doc: Registers a callback to be invoked when a required lock held by another SQLite connection is released. Only available if the library is compiled with SQLITE_ENABLE_UNLOCK_NOTIFY. See the SQLite documentation for full details.

to_generate.name: sqlite_rollback
to_generate.doc: Conflict resolution mode: rollback.

to_generate.name: sqlite_fail
to_generate.doc: Conflict resolution mode: fail the operation.

to_generate.name: sqlite_replace
to_generate.doc: Conflict resolution mode: replace the existing row.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_nloop
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: total number of times that the X-th loop has run.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_nvisit
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: total number of rows examined by all iterations of the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_est
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: query planner estimate for average rows output per loop.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_name
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: name of the index or table used for the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_explain
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN description for the X-th loop.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_selectid
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: unique id for the X-th query plan element.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_parentid
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: id of the parent query element, or zero if none.

to_generate.name: sqlite_scanstat_ncycle
to_generate.doc: Scanstatus option: number of cycles (processor time-stamp counter) for the query element.
A wrapper class for the Fts5ExtensionApi struct, providing access to the SQLite FTS5 extension API struct as an opaque handle.
Opaque handle for a custom FTS5 tokenizer instance.
A wrapper class for the SQLite FTS5 extension API handle.
